commit.message:
fix(screenshot): report onFailure error immediately to backend

Without this, when takeScreenshot() fails (e.g. emulator limitation),
onFailure was logged but no result was POSTed back to the backend.
The backend would then hang for 10s and Railway's proxy would return
a raw 504 before our JSON timeout response.

Fix: postScreenshotResult(null, errorMsg) in onFailure and null-bitmap paths.
Backend screenshot-result now rejects the promise on error field, returning
500 with the device error message instead of a proxy-level 504.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: relaunch app after update instead of just exiting

On macOS, process::exit(0) quits without relaunching. Use app.restart()
so the updated version opens automatically. Also improve banner messages:
"Downloading update..." during download, "Update installed — reopening..."
during the restart delay.

[LDS] Select field component (#73)

Create LemonadeUi.SelectField as a clickable field that reuses

component displays a selected value or placeholder text with a trailing
chevron icon, suitable as a trigger for option lists or pickers.

can be shared across files in the same package.

feat: add persistent memory system for agents

Each Agent gets isolated memory storage (per AgentConfig) that persists
across sessions. Implemented as an in-process MCP tool using the Memory
Tool protocol (view/create/str_replace/insert/delete/rename). Memory is
enabled by default for all agents.

- Backend: MemoryService with path traversal protection, Memory MCP tool,
  REST API for admin management, cascade cleanup on AgentConfig deletion
- Frontend: MemoryManager dialog for viewing/editing memory files,
  purple-themed Memory tool visualization in chat UI
- SDK integration: Memory tool added to allowedTools and autoAllowTools,
  system prompt instructs agent to check memory before each task

commit.message:
fix: dotenv override for system env vars

dotenv doesn't override existing env vars by default. Added
override: true so the .env file takes precedence over any
system-level ANTHROPIC_API_KEY that may exist as empty string.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

extended: add kernel modules, benchmark suite, and test infrastructure

5 kernel modules (C):
- mfs-proto: wire protocol for kernel<->userspace communication
- mfs-kmod: VFS kernel client (mount -t mfs)
- mfsblk: block device driver
- qemu-mfs: QEMU block driver plugin

Rust benchmark suite (mfs-bench):
- 12 benchmark categories: sequential, random, mixed, metadata, small_files,
  large_file, fsync, dir_scale, concurrent, postgres, sqlite, etcd
- Uses fio (posixaio engine) for I/O benchmarks
- JSON output parsing, comparison reports, CSV/table output
- Requires: fio, rustup (1.87+), protobuf-compiler

Label patches (5 patches against MooseFS 4.58.3):
- Per-disk label parsing in mfshdd.cfg
- Disk label reporting from chunkserver to master
- Master placement logic using per-disk labels
- Label precedence configuration
- Config file examples

Test cluster configs for native deployment.
Handover doc for native setup on Blackberry.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: unit conversion system for all physical parameters

Add user-configurable display units for Length (cm/mm/m/in), Area,
Mass (kg/g/t/lb), Volume (L/mL/m3/cm3), Density (g/cm3/kg/m3/t/m3),
and Slump (mm/cm/in). Store always holds backend-native SI units;
conversion is purely presentation-layer. Settings persist in localStorage.

- Create frontend/src/lib/units.ts with types, conversion maps, and
  null-safe toStore*/fromStore* functions
- Add UnitPreferences state + localStorage persistence to Zustand store
- Add unit selector card on /reglages settings page
- Convert container dimension inputs on / page (length/area)
- Convert slump input in SlumpForm
- Convert mass inputs in EssaiForm and RpgEssaiForm
- Convert all outputs in ResultsPanel (mass/volume/density) + Excel export
- Convert saved result display in historique/page.tsx

commit.message:
fix: use relative endpoint in AlarmsAPI.list() to prevent Invalid URL error

AlarmsAPI.list() was constructing the URL with `new URL()` using a
relative path and no base URL, which always throws TypeError. Switched
to the URLSearchParams + relative endpoint string pattern used by every
other API method, so the URL is correctly resolved against baseUrl in
ZebraClient.request().

Bumps @irly-tech/zebra-client to 0.4.1.

commit.message:
fix: don't double-encode macOS .sig file in build workflow

Tauri's macOS build produces a .sig file that is already base64-encoded
(ready for latest.json). Using base64 again double-encodes it, causing
"Invalid encoding in minisign data" in the updater.

Fix: use cat + tr to read the file content directly.

Feat/registry connection (#7)

* WIP: registry connection — client, CLI, dashboard integration

- Registry client in @showrun/core (OAuth Device Flow auth, publish, search, install)
- Token persistence in ~/.config/showrun/auth.json
- CLI `showrun registry` command group (login, logout, whoami, publish, search, install)
- Dashboard /api/registry/* proxy endpoints
- Dashboard RegistryPublishModal with device-flow login
- Publish buttons in PackEditor and PacksView
- Registry API contract document
- Unit tests for registry client

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: CDP browser connection, CLI result storage/query, registry report command

- Add --cdp-url flag to connect to existing Chrome via DevTools Protocol
- Add actionable error messages for missing inputs (prints schema) and
  missing secrets (prints .secrets.json template)
- Auto-store CLI run results to per-pack SQLite (--no-result-store to disable)
- Add showrun results list/query commands with JMESPath filtering
- Add showrun registry report command for pack policy violations
- Add dashboard proxy route for pack reporting
- Post-run reminder about reporting broken/malicious packs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: release script, CDP bringToFront, registry version parsing fix

- Added scripts/release.js for RC workflow (rc, stable, --dry-run)
- CDP connect now uses last page and brings it to front
- Fixed registry client version parsing for nested response format
- Bumped version to 0.1.10-rc.1
- Added AGENT_GUIDE.md and SKILL.md

* feat: scoped @username/slug pack identifiers, extract resolveDefaultPacksDir, remove SKILL.md

Migrate registry client, CLI, and dashboard to use scoped pack refs
(@username/slug) for install, report, and search. Publish keeps using
the short slug (server infers username from auth token). Add packApiPath
helper for URL-safe scoped path segments.

Extract resolveDefaultPacksDir() to @showrun/core so dashboard and CLI
share the same fallback chain (env → local ./taskpacks → system data dir).

Remove SKILL.md (no longer tracked).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: bump to 0.1.10-rc.2, fix release rollback to use in-memory snapshots

Bump all package versions from rc.1 to rc.2. Release script rollback
now snapshots package.json contents before modification and restores
from memory instead of git checkout, preventing multi-version revert
when the working tree has prior uncommitted changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Bump version to 0.1.10-rc.3

* fix: show scoped @username/slug in registry search results

Compose the full scoped identifier from owner.username and slug fields
in search output so users see the installable ref directly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Bump version to 0.1.10-rc.4

Add Blocks puzzle game

Drag-and-drop (tap-to-select + tap-to-place) block puzzle where players
fit 6 coloured pieces onto an irregular 26-cell board. Features:
- 5×6 board with unique shape (extra cell protrudes in row 3)
- 6 pieces: blue 2×3, yellow L-pentomino, green/purple 2×2, cyan bar, red S
- Smart snap: tries all piece-cell alignments for the tapped position
- Easy mode: hint cells pulse to show valid placements
- Hard mode: 5-minute countdown via GameShell
- Elapsed timer + pieces-placed progress counter
- Fully accessible (keyboard navigation, aria-labels)

commit.message:
Fix rejoin, AI stable name, and opponent timeout win

1. Rejoin active game: when a player leaves mid-game (Return to Lobby),
   the game stays active. In the lobby, the ranked match card transforms
   into a rejoin card showing opponent names and a live countdown timer
   (if it's your turn). Clicking rejoins the game from where it left off.

2. Opponent timeout win: if the opponent left and their turn runs out,
   the staying player now automatically wins via a Firestore transaction.
   Uses opponentUidRef to avoid stale closure issues.

3. AI stable username: AI bot always uses 'Player_ai_b' (computed from
   AI_BOT_UID). Name never changes between games. Removed random name
   selection. AI appears in rankings with this consistent name.

commit.message:
Fix BCT nan risk, softmax recomputation, layer_selection validation, double .item()

- BehavioralConsistencyLoss: replace inline JSD with _jsd() helper to avoid
  nan from unclamped .log() on zero probabilities
- BehavioralConsistencyLoss: compute clean_probs/adv_probs once and reuse
  for both loss computation and monitoring metrics
- ActivationConsistencyLoss: raise ValueError for unrecognised layer_selection
  strings instead of silently falling back to "all"
- Trainer: cache loss_dict["loss"].item() to avoid two GPU-CPU syncs per step

Implement comprehensive stats dashboard with charts and visualizations

Added new stats tab layout following recommended design with hybrid approach:
- Summary cards showing total/daily/weekly/anytime completions and current streak

- Best day of week bar chart (Daily goals only)
- Category breakdown doughnut chart (all goals aggregated)
- Personal vs Couple comparison bar chart
- Calendar heatmap showing last 12 weeks of activity
- Achievement badges system with progress tracking

Implemented using Chart.js for visualizations. All colors defined as CSS variables following existing color scheme. Charts use wireframe design aesthetic matching Personal (teal) and Couple (pink/red) tabs.
